title: centaurea kotschyi
intro: Centaurea kotschyi, also known as the Kotschy's knapweed or the small-flowered knapweed, is a perennial plant in the daisy family native to the mountainous regions of the Middle East and the Mediterranean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea kotschyi include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. Centaurea kotschyi can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
